task: What are my high priority tasks in Tasks app? Answer with the titles only. If there are multiples titles, format your answer in a comma separated list.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail icon in the dock. The "Tasks" app is not visible on this screen.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app → | Step 2: Navigate to the high priority tasks list | Step 3: Extract the titles of the high priority tasks | Step 4: Format the titles as a comma-separated list and output

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the Tasks app is not on the current home screen page. Since the goal is to find high priority tasks within the Tasks app, I need to launch it first. I will use the system's open app function to launch "Tasks" directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen displays the "My Tasks" list in the Tasks app. There are several tasks visible like "Renew Driver's License", "Submit Expense Report", "Water Plants", etc., with different colors (blue, yellow) possibly indicating different priorities or lists, but no explicit "High Priority" filter or list is visible on this main screen. The bottom navigation bar has a menu icon (hamburger menu) on the far left, a central add button, and other icons on the right.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the high priority tasks list → | Step 3: Extract the titles of the high priority tasks | Step 4: Format the titles as a comma-separated list and output

[Decision]
To find the "high priority tasks", I need to access the app's filtering or navigation options since they are not directly in the "My Tasks" view. The standard way to find different lists or filters in this app is through the side menu. Therefore, I will tap the hamburger menu icon at the bottom left to look for a "High Priority" list or filter option.

[Memory]
App opened. Need to find "High Priority" tasks list/filter. Current view is "My Tasks".

[Observation]
The screen displays an open navigation drawer menu in the Tasks app. Under the "Filters" section, there is an option labeled "High Priority" which shows 1 task next to it. Other options include "My Tasks" (14 tasks), "Today" (12 tasks), "Recently modified" (7 tasks), and lists under "Local lists" like "Default list", "Donate", etc.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the high priority tasks list ✓ | Step 3: Extract the titles of the high priority tasks → | Step 4: Format the titles as a comma-separated list and output

[Decision]
Based on the current plan to extract the titles of high priority tasks, I first need to view the list itself. The "High Priority" filter is clearly visible in the menu under Filters. I will tap on "High Priority" to switch the view to this specific list.

[Memory]
Navigated to the menu. "High Priority" filter identified and contains 1 task. Next step is to read the title of this task once the list is displayed.

[Observation]
The screen displays the "High Priority" list. There is exactly one task visible on the screen with the title "Prepare agenda for weekly meeting" and a subtext "Double-check calculations." The task is unchecked.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the high priority tasks list ✓ | Step 3: Extract the titles of the high priority tasks ✓ | Step 4: Format the titles as a comma-separated list and output →

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the high priority list contains only one task. The title of this task is "Prepare agenda for weekly meeting". Since the plan's current step is to format and output the titles as requested, I will provide this title as the final answer to the user's query, as there are no other tasks to include in a comma-separated list.

[Memory]
High Priority list contains 1 task. Task title identified: "Prepare agenda for weekly meeting". Ready to output.
